question: Select A, B, C, or D. For the reaction: H2O (g) + CO (g) <-> H2 (g) + CO2 (g) consider an experiment where equal numbers of moles H2O, CO, CO2, and H2 are injected into a rigid, sealed reaction vessel and heated to 500K. If Kc = 24 at 500 K, which of the following correctly describes the composition of the equilibrium mixture? a) [H20] = [CO] = [H2] = [CO2]: b) [H20] = [CO] < [H2] = [CO2]: c) [H20] = [CO] > [H2] = [CO2]. Give your answer in terms of A, B, C, or D.
system_instructions:
You are helping with a homework problem.

- Keep the explanation short (a few sentences).

If Kc = 24 at 500 K, which of the following correctly describes the composition of the equilibrium mixture? a) [H20] = [CO] = [H2] = [CO2]: b) [H20] = [CO] < [H2] = [CO2]: c) [H20] = [CO] > [H2] = [CO2]. Initial Q = 1 < Kc = 24, so reaction shifts right producing products. Let x be extent: [H2]=[CO2]=c0+x and [H2O]=[CO]=c0-x, giving products > reactants at equilibrium.

Initial Q = 1 < Kc = 24, so the reaction shifts right. Let initial concentration = c0 and extent = x, then Kc = ((c0+x)/(c0-x))^2 = 24 so (c0+x)/(c0-x) = sqrt(24) > 1, hence x > 0 and [H2]=[CO2] > [H2O]=[CO].  
then the answer: B

Start with equal concentrations c0 so Q = 1 < Kc = 24; reaction shifts right. Let x be extent: [H2]=[CO2]=c0+x and [H2O]=[CO]=c0−x. Kc = ((c0+x)/(c0−x))^2 = 24, so (c0+x)/(c0−x)=√24 ≈ 4.899, giving x = c0(√24−1)/(√24+1) ≈ 0.661c0 > 0. Thus [H2]=[CO2] > [H2O]=[CO].  
then the answer: B

Qc = 1 < Kc = 24, so reaction shifts right. Let initial concentration = c0 and extent = x: [H2]=[CO2]=c0+x, [H2O]=[CO]=c0−x. Kc = ((c0+x)/(c0−x))^2 = 24 ⇒ (1 + x/c0)/(1 − x/c0) = √24 ≈ 4.899, so x/c0 = (√24 − 1)/(√24 + 1) ≈ 0.661 > 0. Hence [H2]=[CO2] > [H2O]=[CO], choice B.  
then the answer: B
